stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML topics including transformers, llama, and text-generation.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking around a significant open-source LLM.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
The model is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: OpenAI just released its answer to Claude Mythos
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: OpenAI is launching 'Daybreak,' a new AI initiative focused on proactive cybersecurity.
Daybreak utilizes the Codex Security AI agent to build threat models from organizational code.
The system automates the validation and detection of potential software vulnerabilities before exploitation.

stories.title: Apple brings encrypted RCS chats to iPhone
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Apple has integrated end-to-end encrypted RCS messaging into the Messages app on iOS.
This feature allows iPhone users to have secure, encrypted chats with Android users.
The update, part of iOS 26.5, ensures that neither Apple nor Google can view the message content.

stories.title: Google stopped a zero-day hack that it says was developed with AI
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Google successfully identified and neutralized a zero-day exploit.
The exploit was reportedly developed using Artificial Intelligence by cyber threat actors.
The attack aimed for a 'mass exploitation event' capable of bypassing two-factor authentication.

stories.title: The Memory Curse: How Expanded Recall Erodes Cooperative Intent in LLM Agents
stories.author: Vincent Conitzer
bullet_points: The study introduces the 'memory curse,' finding that expanding LLM context windows systematically degrades cooperation in multi-agent social dilemmas.
Across 7 LLMs and 4 games, expanded history was shown to erode cooperation in 18 out of 28 tested settings.
The research isolates the mechanism, linking the breakdown in cooperation to the erosion of forward-looking intent in the LLMs' reasoning traces.

stories.title: Postmortem: TanStack npm supply-chain compromise
stories.author: varunsharma07
bullet_points: The story details a postmortem analysis of a supply-chain compromise affecting the TanStack npm package.
This highlights critical vulnerabilities in the modern JavaScript/npm ecosystem.
It serves as a major cautionary tale regarding dependency security and software integrity.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, DeepSeek-R1, on the Hugging Face platform.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a large language model.
The tags suggest relevance to transformer architectures and the DeepSeek family of models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content references the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It highlights the use of standard NLP pipelines, specifically 'text-generation'.
Key associated technologies include transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing AI workloads on Mac devices.
This is presented as a preview feature, indicating ongoing development and performance improvements.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being deployed in partnership with OpenAI and ROOST.
The models are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting open-source safety tooling.

stories.title: Data center guzzled 30 million gallons of water and nobody noticed for months
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Data centers are consuming massive amounts of water (30 million gallons) without public awareness.
The article questions whether AI itself can solve the escalating water consumption problem of the AI industry.
The outlook presented is pessimistic regarding sustainable water usage in the rapidly growing AI sector.

stories.title: Rubric-Grounded RL: Structured Judge Rewards for Generalizable Reasoning
stories.author: Dan O'Malley
bullet_points: Introduces 'rubric-grounded reinforcement learning' (RL) to improve AI reasoning.
The core innovation is decomposing the reward signal into multiple, verifiable, task-specific criteria.
Uses a frozen LLM judge to provide a partial-credit optimization signal instead of a single binary score.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for advanced AI features within Claude Code.
The new capabilities include the implementation of subagents for complex task decomposition.
Web search functionality has been added to enhance the contextual awareness of Claude Code.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI assistance for coding tasks.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated compatibility with the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local model deployment and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-weight models.
Codex, when integrated, can read, modify, and execute code within a user's working directory.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Yarbo says it will remove the intentional backdoor from its robot lawn mower
stories.author: Sean Hollister
bullet_points: Yarbo is removing a previously noted intentional remote backdoor from its robot lawn mower.
The backdoor access, which could allow unauthorized reprogramming, is being eliminated.
Customers will now have the option to choose whether the remote feature is installed.

stories.title: Here’s what Mira Murati’s AI company is up to
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Mira Murati's AI company, Thinking Machines, is developing 'interaction models.'
These models aim to enable natural, collaborative interaction between humans and AI.
The technology involves continuously processing inputs like audio and video.

stories.title: Linux bitten by second severe vulnerability in as many weeks
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Linux has recently experienced a second severe vulnerability within a short timeframe.
Production-version patches are currently being released to address these security flaws.
Users are strongly advised to install the available patches immediately for security.

stories.title: Starlink shuts down its GPS-style cheat code. Researchers may unlock it anyway.
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Starlink has disabled a specific GPS-like location feature.
The shutdown is unlikely to stop research into alternative location tracking methods.
The story highlights ongoing interest in non-standard GPS solutions.

stories.title: EmambaIR: Efficient Visual State Space Model for Event-guided Image Reconstruction
stories.author: Yunhang Qian
bullet_points: EmambaIR introduces a new Visual State Space Model (VSSM) for event-guided image reconstruction.
It aims to overcome limitations of existing methods, specifically the global correlation issues of CNNs and the quadratic complexity of ViTs.
The model is designed to be computationally efficient for high-resolution image processing.

stories.title: Flow-OPD: On-Policy Distillation for Flow Matching Models
stories.author: Feng Zhao
bullet_points: Introduces Flow-OPD, a novel method applying On-Policy Distillation (OPD) to Flow Matching (FM) models.
Addresses critical bottlenecks in FM text-to-image models, specifically reward sparsity and gradient interference during multi-task alignment.
Aims to mitigate the 'seesaw effect' and reward hacking issues common when optimizing heterogeneous objectives in generative models.

stories.title: CA-SQL: Complexity-Aware Inference Time Reasoning for Text-to-SQL via Exploration and Compute Budget Allocation
stories.author: Nianwen Xue
bullet_points: Introduces CA-SQL, a new Text-to-SQL pipeline designed to improve performance on challenging benchmarks like Bird-Bench.
Addresses the limitation of current LLM solutions by focusing on inadequate solution space exploration during inference.
CA-SQL utilizes complexity-aware reasoning, involving exploration and compute budget allocation to find better candidate queries.

stories.title: Fast Byte Latent Transformer
stories.author: Srinivasan Iyer
bullet_points: The Byte Latent Transformer (BLT) addresses the slow generation speed of byte-level language models (LMs).
BLT introduces BLT Diffusion (BLT-D), a new variant trained with a block-wise diffusion objective.
This approach aims to maintain the performance of byte-level models while overcoming the bottleneck of slow, byte-by-byte autoregressive generation.

stories.title: SCOPE: Structured Decomposition and Conditional Skill Orchestration for Complex Image Generation
stories.author: Feng Zhao
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of faithfully realizing complex visual intents in text-to-image models.
Introduces the concept of 'semantic commitments' that must be tracked across grounding, generation, and verification.
Formalizes the 'Conceptual Rift' as the failure to maintain identifiable operational units throughout the generation lifecycle.

stories.title: Beyond Pairs: Your Language Model is Secretly Optimizing a Preference Graph
stories.author: Shervin Malmasi
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation of Direct Preference Optimization (DPO), which currently relies on pairwise comparisons for alignment.
It proposes a method to exploit richer preference structures present when training data includes multiple rollouts per prompt.
The core issue with standard DPO is that collapsing multi-rollout data into pairs discards transitivity and can cause training instability.

stories.title: Normalizing Trajectory Models
stories.author: Josh Susskind
bullet_points: NTM addresses limitations in few-step diffusion models where coarse transitions break the Gaussian denoising assumption.
The proposed Normalizing Trajectory Models (NTM) model each reverse step using an expressive conditional normalizing flow.
NTM maintains an exact likelihood training framework, unlike existing few-step methods that sacrifice it.

stories.title: Zero-Shot Imagined Speech Decoding via Imagined-to-Listened MEG Mapping
stories.author: Shihab Shamma
bullet_points: Proposes a novel method to decode imagined speech from non-invasive MEG recordings.
Addresses the challenge of scarce and poorly aligned imagined speech datasets.
Leverages paired recordings (listening vs. imagining) from trained musicians to improve decoding accuracy.

stories.title: Fast Byte Latent Transformer
stories.author: Srinivasan Iyer
bullet_points: Addresses the slow generation bottleneck in byte-level language models (LMs).
Introduces the Byte Latent Transformer (BLT) with novel training and generation techniques.
Features BLT Diffusion (BLT-D), a fast variant trained with an auxiliary block-wise diffusion objective.

stories.title: Beyond Pairs: Your Language Model is Secretly Optimizing a Preference Graph
stories.author: Shervin Malmasi
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation of Direct Preference Optimization (DPO), which currently relies on pairwise comparisons for aligning language models.
It proposes a method to exploit richer preference structures present in training data that contains multiple rollouts per prompt.
The core problem DPO faces is that collapsing multi-rollout data into pairs discards transitivity and can cause unstable training.

stories.title: Don't Get Your Kroneckers in a Twist: Gaussian Processes on High-Dimensional Incomplete Grids
stories.author: Ove Christiansen
bullet_points: Introduces CUTS-GPR, a novel method for numerically exact Gaussian Process Regression (GPR) in high-dimensional spaces.
The method achieves near-linear or linear scaling with training data size ($N$) and low-order polynomial scaling with dimensionality ($D$).
This efficiency is derived from combining an additive kernel with an incomplete grid and exploiting the resulting kernel matrix structure.

stories.title: PropSplat: Map-Free RF Field Reconstruction via 3D Gaussian Propagation Splatting
stories.author: Joseph Camp
bullet_points: PropSplat introduces a map-free method for reconstructing Radio Frequency (RF) fields.
It utilizes 3D anisotropic Gaussian primitives to model propagation, bypassing the need for detailed 3D maps or extensive measurements.
This approach addresses the high cost and infeasibility of traditional site-specific propagation modeling in rapid deployment scenarios.

stories.title: UCLA discovers first stroke rehabilitation drug to repair brain damage (2025)
stories.author: bookofjoe
bullet_points: UCLA researchers have discovered a potential new drug for stroke rehabilitation.
The drug is designed to actively repair brain damage caused by strokes.
The news originates from Hacker News, suggesting a scientific/research focus.

stories.title: I Was About to Rewrite My Chat Router. The Bug Was Two Lines in a Prompt.
stories.author: alimafana
bullet_points: A seemingly complex architectural bug in a chat router was traced back to a simple prompt instruction.
The issue demonstrated how LLM behavior can mask underlying data retrieval success, leading to misdiagnosis.
The fix involved a minor change to a variable label within the prompt, highlighting the sensitivity of LLM interactions.
